summary: Resets the live project shape to a clean dtk-template-style pipeline (configure.py/ninja/generated report.json), moving nearly 1,000 files of the prior campaign's custom tooling, docs, and a PC-port experiment wholesale into archive/previous_campaign/ while preserving recovered symbol names in the active symbols.txt.
decomp_lessons: When resetting a repo to a template pipeline, moving prior work into an archive/ subdirectory (rather than deleting it) preserves it as source material for later reclaim PRs (see PRs 21-23) instead of losing it.

Keeping the active symbols.txt (recovered names) while stripping out old split/report/config variants lets naming knowledge survive a structural reset even though object/split data gets rebuilt from scratch.
This is a foundational reset PR: it explains why PRs 21-24 exist as separate 'reclaim from archive' follow-ups rather than one big migration.
smart_moves: Validated the reset with the full pipeline (configure.py --no-progress, ninja all_source, ninja main build, progress report) plus an explicit scan for stray .inc/inline-asm before merging, and confirmed CI (build workflow) passed.
naming_conventions: Retained recovered function/symbol names in config/GC6E01/symbols.txt across the reset even though the underlying source tree was rebuilt from the dtk-template.

key_files.role: 124 lines added / 102 removed, rebuilding object registration to match the dtk-template baseline
1465 lines changed, reformatted/realigned to the reset template while keeping recovered names
181 lines added, rebuilt split ranges for the reset baseline
Rewritten (30 lines vs prior 445) to describe the new dtk-template-based workflow for future agents
Rewritten around the reset baseline pipeline
New archive root holding the entire prior campaign's tooling, docs, and PC-port experiment (~970 moved files)
